resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def mergeAlternately(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> str:
        merged = ''
        i = 0
        j = 0
        while i < len(word1) or j < len(word2):
            if i < len(word1):
                merged += word1[i]
                i += 1
            if j < len(word2):
                merged += word2[j]
                j += 1
        return merged
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through both input strings simultaneously, appending characters in alternating order to a new string. This approach ensures all characters are processed efficiently, resulting in linear time complexity. The space complexity is also linear due to the creation of the merged string.
